title: Iteration control and quality signals
purpose: Extend the agent loop with runtime monitors that detect when the agent is approaching resource limits, looping on the same error, or producing low-quality output. These monitors observe the loop state and inject guidance or emit warnings — they do not control loop termination (that remains with the schema budget and max-iterations from N-005).
task: Implement iteration monitors in the loop/iteration.rs module. Four monitors: (1) token threshold detection that tracks cumulative usage against a configurable context window size and emits a warning event when a threshold percentage is crossed, (2) soft handoff that injects wrap-up guidance into the conversation when a second (higher) threshold is crossed, (3) repeated failure detection that identifies when the agent is looping on the same or structurally similar errors across consecutive iterations, (4) semantic quality signals that scan assistant text for hedging language and premature completion patterns. All monitors are called from the agent loop after each iteration. Testing: unit tests for each monitor independently, integration test via MockProvider showing the loop responding to threshold crossing.

requirements.title: Define iteration monitor types and configuration
requirements.spec: The iteration module SHALL define an IterationMonitorConfig struct with fields: context_window_tokens (u64 — total tokens available), warn_threshold_pct (f64, 0.0-1.0 — percentage at which to emit warning), handoff_threshold_pct (f64, 0.0-1.0 — percentage at which to inject handoff guidance), handoff_guidance (String — the wrap-up message to inject), failure_repeat_window (usize — number of recent errors to compare for loop detection), hedging_patterns (Vec<String> — regex patterns for hedging detection). It SHALL define an IterationSignal enum with variants: None, TokenWarning { used: u64, limit: u64, pct: f64 }, HandoffTriggered { used: u64, limit: u64, guidance: String }, RepeatedFailure { error_signature: String, consecutive_count: usize }, QualityWarning { signals: Vec<QualitySignal> }. QualitySignal SHALL be an enum with variants Hedging { matched_pattern: String, text_excerpt: String } and PrematureCompletion { text_excerpt: String }.
acceptance: IterationMonitorConfig is a public struct with all specified fields
IterationMonitorConfig derives Clone and Debug
IterationSignal is a public enum with all specified variants
QualitySignal is a public enum with Hedging and PrematureCompletion variants
All types exported from the loop module

requirements.title: Implement token threshold detection
requirements.spec: A function check_token_threshold SHALL accept cumulative Usage and IterationMonitorConfig. It SHALL compute the percentage of context_window_tokens consumed (input_tokens + output_tokens). If the percentage crosses handoff_threshold_pct, return IterationSignal::HandoffTriggered. Else if it crosses warn_threshold_pct, return IterationSignal::TokenWarning. Otherwise return IterationSignal::None. The handoff threshold takes precedence — if both are crossed, only HandoffTriggered is returned.
acceptance: Usage at 50% with warn=0.8 -> None
Usage at 85% with warn=0.8, handoff=0.95 -> TokenWarning
Usage at 96% with warn=0.8, handoff=0.95 -> HandoffTriggered (not TokenWarning)
Usage at 0 -> None
context_window_tokens=0 -> None (no division by zero)

requirements.title: Implement soft handoff guidance injection
requirements.spec: When check_token_threshold returns HandoffTriggered, the handoff_guidance string from the config SHALL be injected. The iteration module SHALL expose a function format_handoff_message that takes the HandoffTriggered signal and returns a formatted string suitable for injection as a UserMessage. The message SHALL include the guidance text, the current usage percentage, and an instruction to summarize progress and prepare for continuation. The handoff SHALL fire at most once per agent step — an IterationMonitorState struct SHALL track whether handoff has already been triggered.
acceptance: format_handoff_message returns a string containing the guidance text
format_handoff_message includes usage percentage in the message
format_handoff_message includes instruction to summarize and prepare for continuation
IterationMonitorState tracks handoff_fired: bool
Second call at same threshold does not re-trigger handoff
TokenWarning still fires after handoff has fired (they are independent signals)

requirements.title: Implement repeated failure detection
requirements.spec: The iteration module SHALL define a function check_repeated_failures that takes a slice of recent error strings (from tool errors or schema validation failures) and a window size. It SHALL compute an error signature by normalizing error strings (trim whitespace, lowercase, strip numeric literals) and comparing consecutive entries. If the same normalized signature appears in N or more consecutive positions (where N = failure_repeat_window), return IterationSignal::RepeatedFailure with the signature and count. IterationMonitorState SHALL maintain a Vec<String> of recent error signatures.
acceptance: 3 identical errors with window=3 -> RepeatedFailure
3 identical errors with window=4 -> None
Errors differing only in line numbers normalize to same signature
'Error at line 5: foo' and 'Error at line 12: foo' match after normalization
Empty error list -> None
Mixed different errors -> None

requirements.title: Implement semantic quality signal detection
requirements.spec: The iteration module SHALL define a function check_quality_signals that takes assistant response text and the hedging_patterns from config. Hedging detection: each pattern in hedging_patterns is compiled as a regex and matched against the text. Matches produce QualitySignal::Hedging with the matched pattern and a surrounding text excerpt (up to 80 chars centered on the match). Premature completion detection: if the text is shorter than 50 characters and contains terminal phrases ('I hope this helps', 'Let me know if', 'Is there anything else'), produce QualitySignal::PrematureCompletion. If any signals are found, return IterationSignal::QualityWarning.
acceptance: Text 'I think maybe this could work' with pattern 'I think maybe' -> Hedging signal
Text 'Let me know if you need more' (28 chars) -> PrematureCompletion signal
Text 'The function returns 42.' with no matching patterns -> None
Multiple hedging patterns matched -> QualityWarning with multiple signals
Long text (>50 chars) with 'Let me know if' -> no PrematureCompletion (only short text triggers)
Empty hedging_patterns list -> only premature completion checked

requirements.title: Define IterationMonitorState and evaluate_iteration
requirements.spec: IterationMonitorState SHALL be a struct tracking: handoff_fired (bool), recent_errors (Vec<String>), warn_fired (bool). A public function evaluate_iteration SHALL accept &mut IterationMonitorState, cumulative Usage, the latest assistant text (Option<&str>), latest errors (Option<&[String]>), and &IterationMonitorConfig. It SHALL run all four checks in order (token threshold, repeated failure, quality signals) and return a Vec<IterationSignal> containing all non-None signals from this iteration. This is the single entry point the agent loop calls after each iteration.
acceptance: evaluate_iteration returns empty vec when nothing triggers
evaluate_iteration returns multiple signals when token warning + quality warning both fire
State persists across calls — handoff only fires once
Errors are appended to recent_errors across calls
warn_fired prevents duplicate TokenWarning signals

requirements.title: Add regex dependency
requirements.spec: Cargo.toml SHALL add the regex crate as a dependency for norn. Used by hedging pattern matching in quality signal detection.
acceptance: Cargo.toml includes regex dependency for norn
cargo check -p norn succeeds

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the agent loop's termination conditions — schema budget and max-iterations (N-005) are unchanged
SHALL NOT implement context compaction or summarization in response to threshold signals — that is a separate concern
SHALL NOT implement automatic retry or recovery from repeated failures — signals are advisory
SHALL NOT implement inbound channel integration (C20-C22)
SHALL NOT hardcode hedging patterns — all patterns come from configuration
SHALL NOT require integration with runner.rs in this brief — integration into the loop is a follow-up wiring task
verification: cargo check -p norn succeeds with zero errors
cargo test -p norn — all iteration tests pass
cargo clippy -p norn -- -D warnings zero errors
Token threshold tests cover: below warn, between warn and handoff, above handoff, zero window, re-trigger suppression
Repeated failure tests cover: exact match, normalized match, below window, empty list
Quality signal tests cover: hedging match, premature completion, no match, multiple signals
grep -rn 'unwrap()\|expect(' crates/norn/src/loop/iteration.rs returns no matches outside tests
